Video.summary: “The Century Race”: Dale believes he found the bike that will help him beat a rival in an annual race of 100 year old motorcycles. But will it run?
Video.title: The Century Race

Video.summary: A California collector gambles on a big payday from his Crocker/Caddy combo.
Video.title: Doubling Down

Video.summary: For Dale and Matt sometimes the dotted line to treasure splits and heads in opposite directions. In this edition of What’s in the Barn, time is thrust to the forefront of this multi-state, long distance, north, south search. Timeless and priceless 110 year old bikes, antique cars, airplanes, and the infamous Egberts 1930 neon Harley-Davidson sign that coined the phrase “Ride a Winner” are high on this father and son’s list of “must haves”. The clock ticks, both literally and figuratively, on one rare time piece and two old-timer’s cars, bikes, and automobiles.
Video.title: Time is Not On Their Side

Image.alt: Time is Not On Their Side
Time is Not On Their Side
Time is Not On Their Side

Video.summary: Promoters of the prestigious Amelia Island Concours have called on Dale to develop a show and field bikes for an “Orphan Bike” show. They have defined orphan bike as extremely rare, that aren't a Harley nor Indian machines.
Video.title: Orphan Bikes

Video.summary: “A Motorcycle Crime Story”: The horrific tale of a double murder over money and motorcycles.
Video.title: Motorcycle Crime Story

Video.summary: The RM Auction Company has worked with Dale before as a buyer, seller, historian, and caretaker at the prestigious Lee Hartung auction. But this event is no last minute rush to define one man’s treasures. This is a well calculated, time consuming, multi-media, and multi motorcycle and prized art deal.
Video.title: Second Time's a Charm

Video.summary: Home Depot Senior Vice President, Gordon Erickson, is a huge fan of Wheels Through Time. He is also a huge supporter of the Wounded Warrior program. For years the hardware giant has been donating goods and services to help returning servicemen with their fix-it-up projects. Now they would like to go one step further and build a WWII military bike to raise awareness and funds. Only problem is where to get one? Enter Dale and an exhaustive, time sensitive, cross country search for a rare military machine.
Video.title: Wounded Warrior Project

Video.summary: “Yes, Dale there is a 100 Year Old Harley”: Dale hopes his labor of love becomes a prized possession when a six-figure Harley goes to auction.
Video.title: 100 Year Old Harley

Video.summary: A thousand bulging barns of choppers, bobbers, and board trackers are emptied and on display in the Quad Cities.
Video.title: Davenport Swap

Video.summary: A vague tip leads Matt and Dale into a WWII era military warehouse and beyond.
Video.title: Peashooter

Video.summary: A cross-country race to lay claim to some of the world’s most sought after works specials.
Video.title: Hidden Hillclimbers

Video.summary: “12hrs. & Running”: Dale turns a surprise 60th birthday party into a challenge to his guests to start 120 of the rarest motorcycles in the world.
Video.title: 12 Hours and Running

Video.summary: An family farm in the mid-west contains houses, barns, outbuildings, trailers, grain silos and buses full of old stuff and perhaps the biggest stash of undiscovered old motorcycles in the country. The collection was put together by their unnamed father. Seller agrees to have Dale broker rare Excelsior for high-end client. Dale has the buyer, but will he outbid his own client for the exclusive find?
Video.title: Diamond in Dodge City

Video.summary: Matt is out for revenge. Last year Dale beat him badly at the Annual Vintage bike race in Barber, Alabama. But this year there is a whole new tale to tell. With just days until the race - Dale is secretly struggling to get the bike he beat Matt with last year up and working. Matt struggles just to locate a 100-year old bike. Matt's prayers are answered thanks to an old Catholic Priest he located who has possession of a bike. The priest might let Matt have the bike to race - but there are conditions. The toughest one: Don't tell Dale you have it. Can Matt afford to buy the bike and get it running in time for the race without Dale seeing it? Will Dale even get his broken bike started? And if they ever get to race, will Matt exact his revenge?
Video.title: Don't Tell Dale

Video.summary: Built in the mid-60’s by eccentric engineer “Wild Bill” Gelbke, the Road Dog was, and still is, the largest motorcycle ever built. It utilized aircraft tubing, Corvette disc brakes, and a Chevy truck differential to keep it stable. The one-off creation disappeared when Gelbke did in 1978. Outflanked once when the bike was spotted and sold to a private collector in 1995, Dale has vowed to locate the machine and make Wheels Through Time its final resting place.
Video.title: Biker Lore

Video.summary: Big Time Motorcycle Auction
Video.title: Vegas Payday

Video.summary: Nick Rutter walks you through step-by-step on how to remove stubborn bug splatter from your vehicle along with tips and techniques for using a waterless wash as well as a clay bar.
Video.title: Family Truckster Restoration Part 1

Video.summary: Nick Rutter provides tips and techniques for restoring dull, faded paint using a forced-rotation dual action polisher along with a graphene 3-in-1 ceramic polish, along with severe scratch and scuff removal.
Video.title: Family Truckster Restoration Part 2

Video.summary: A 2000 Silverado comes into the shop with a recurring cooling problem. The guys put their detective hats on and find the root of the problem.
Video.title: Cooling Maintenance

Video.summary: The guys learn what it takes to finish King of the Hammers with the ESAB sponsored Ultra Four race truck in the shop.
Video.title: Let’s Roll

Video.summary: Kevin and Willie B. highlight the inner workings of the EcoBoost engine and show how it’s changing the game on the racetrack. Plus, Kevin gets a rare look behind the doors of Ford Racing’s dyno facility.
Video.title: Ford EcoBoost

Video.summary: Willie B. and Kevin do some detective work on a ‘55 Nomad that may be the next addition to Willie’s collection. Meanwhile, a Jeep Wrangler is in the shop for an illuminating upgrade.
Video.title: How to Buy a Classic Car

Image.alt: How to Buy a Classic Car
How to Buy a Classic Car
How to Buy a Classic Car

Video.summary: Kevin heads to the world-renowned Pratt & Miller Engineering Headquarters for a closer look at the company’s upcoming projects and legendary past.
Video.title: Pratt & Miller

Video.summary: Kevin and Willie B. tap into the pent-up power of a Ford F-150 EcoBoost by adding a new water-methanol injection system, intake, and exhaust before strapping it to the dyno.
Video.title: EcoBoost Unleashed

Video.summary: Willie B. and Kevin bring in a high-mileage truck for a routine check-up and walk us through some repairs every car owner should know.
Video.title: 120,000 Mile Maintenance

Video.summary: Kevin and Willie B. take on the challenge of outfitting a Jeep for a rental company whose customer base isn’t known for driving cautiously.
Video.title: Moab Jeep

Video.summary: Just when things couldn't get more stressful, Mark agrees to finally get the 1930 Chevy Coach fully restored by the end of the week. Meanwhile, Mark QC's the next cars in the process of resurrection.
Video.title: Put Me In, Coach

Video.summary: See the complete 3-year restoration of Kimberly Cook's 1970 Barracuda Convertible: the body, paint, engine, and convertible top, culminating in Graveyard Carz' most emotional reveal to date.
Video.title: How to Cook a Barracuda

Video.summary: New shop. New crew. An All-new Graveyard Carz! The ghouls tackle a 1970 Cuda.
Video.title: Commando Cuda vs. Magnum Force

Video.summary: Mark paints a Hemi Charger in his favorite Mopar color, burnt orange. AMD delivers the Phantom Cuda, and the ghouls finish the Dailey's 1970 Cuda 383, a project four years in the making.
Video.title: Rallye Red Restored

Video.summary: The ghouls wrap up the 340 Cuda and install panels on the Hemi Charger. Mark trains Allysa on O.E.M. assembly line markings. The legendary red Plymouth Fury "Christine" visits the shop.
Video.title: Hell Hath No '58 Fury

Video.summary: The ghouls have problems with after-market parts for the 400 Magnum V8. Allysa begins working in the shop, and two rare Mopars are disassembled
Video.title: 400 Magnum Misfire

Video.summary: Mark's coffee addiction is spotlighted, and the ghouls work on a 1970 Challenger.
Video.title: Return of the '70 Sunroof Challenger

Video.summary: Work on a 1970 Cuda 340 is featured.
Video.title: 70 'Cuda Grace
